title: Model resources around stable nouns
description: Shape the API around the domain's persistent entities and their relationships, not around the actions a particular screen needs today.
body: Use when defining a new API surface or extending one. Do not invent RPC-style verbs that mirror a single UI flow. Actions: identify the domain entities and their relationships; expose them as resources with consistent identifiers; map operations to standard methods on those resources. Validation signals include a consumer navigating the model from resource names alone and new clients reusing the same resources. Risk boundary: action-shaped endpoints fragment the model and are hard to evolve, so anchor the design on durable nouns and review additions for consistency.
